VERY LARGE HAIL, PERHAPS BASEBALL SIZE, WAS HEARD HITTING THE HOUSE. THE TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
A LARGE COTTONWOOD TREE WAS SNAPPED, TWISTED,BROKEN AND SPREAD AROUND THE RANCH YARD. A STACK OF LUMBER WAS LIFTED UP AND TOSSED AROUND THE YARD. THE TIME OF THE REPORT WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A TORNADO REPORTED 4 MILES NORTH OF ANTIOCH. POWER LINES WERE ALSO REPORTED TO BE DOWNED NEAR THE LOCATION.

NEDOR SITE 804 ON HIGHWAY 83
RELAYED TO SHERIFF DEPARTMENT LARGE TREE UPROOTED. WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED AT 60 TO 70 MPH.
RELAYED FROM OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE, NEIGHBOR HAD BAY WINDOW BLOWN OUT BY LARGE TREE LIMB. ESTIMATED 70 MPH WINDS.
RELAYED FROM OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE, NEIGHBOR HAD BAY WINDOW BROKEN BY LARGE TREE LIMB. ESTIMATED 70 MPH WINDS.
DELAYED REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF DAMAGE TO AN EMPTY GRAIN BIN. ESTIMATED TIME OF 830 PM CDT.
RELAYED FROM EMERGENCY MANAGER THAT 8 INCH PLUS TREE BRANCH DOWN. ESTIMATED WIND GUST OF 70 MPH.
STAPLETON FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED 8 TO 10 INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS DOWN. WINDS MAY HAVE BEEN IN EXCESS OF 70MPH.

SHED DESTROYED AND SCATTERED 60 YARDS BEHIND THE FOUNDATION OF WHERE IT ONCE STOOD. THE BOTTOM PLATE WAS BOLTED INTO THE FOUNDATION BUT THE BOTTOM OF THE STUDS LOOKED PARTIALLY ROTTED AND WERE STRAIGHT NAILED INTO THE BOTTOM PLATE. THE SHED ALSO HAD LARGE GARAGE DOORS. ESTIMATED 85 MPH WINDS.
EST 105MPH POWER POLES SNAPPED. POLES SHOWED MINIMAL TO MODERATE WEAR SO REDUCED BASELINE ESTIMATE FROM 118MPH TO 105MPH
EST 118MPH WINDS. ONE QUARTER MILE OF NEW POWER POLES SNAPPED IN HALF AND TOSSED INTO THE ADJACENT FIELD. ONE POLE WAS ALSO ANCHORED BY ITS CONNECTION TO A PIVOT.
EST 70 MPH. FLIPPED EMPTY PIVOT AND EMPTY TRAILER. METAL BUILDING SYSTEM SUSTAINED DAMAGE TO SLIDING DOORS AND HAD THE SKY LIGHTS POPPED OUT AND CARRIED AWAY.
EST 90 MPH. WOOD POWER POLES LEANING OVER

EST 75MPH. EMPTY 5TH WHEEL CAMPER WAS BLOWN OVER AND PUSHED INTO THE CORNER OF A HOME CAUSING DAMAGE TO THE SIDING AND THE ROOF OVERHANG. AN ANCHORED WOODEN SWING SET WAS ALSO BLOWN OVER AND A BASKETBALL HOOPE WITH WEIGHTED BASE BLOWN 20 YARDS.

EST 80 MPH. PARTIAL LOSS OF ROOF COVERING ON AN MBS BUILDING. PARITALLY FILLED LARGE WATER TANKS BLOWN OVER AT A FEED LOT. POWER POLES DOWN, ONE LINE CAME DOWN ON HWY 92, SNAGGED A CAR, AND WAS DRUG FOR ONE HALF MILE. THIS CONTRIBUTED TO THE DEGREE OF DAMAGE TO THE POLES. TREE BRANCH FALLING ON POWER LINE LIKELY CAUSED THE POLES TO LEAN INITIALLY.
EST 75MPH. SEVERAL LARGE 10 INCH LIMBS DOWN FROM SOFTWOOD TREES.
EST 70 MPH. FLIPPED EMPTY PIVOT
EST 75MPH. SEVERAL LARGE 10 TO 12 INCH LIMBS DOWN AT A RESIDENCE. ONE FELL ON A POWER LINE AND DRAPED IT INTO A ROAD. ANOTHER LANDED ON A HORSE TRAILER AND DENTED THE TOP.
EST 75MPH. WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE ACROSS THE REC AREA WITH LIMBS UP TO 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER DOWN.
EST 100MPH. NEWER WELL CONSTRUCTED AND ANCHORED METAL BUILDING HAD DOORS BLOWN IN AND PART OF THE ROOF PEELED OFF AND BLOWN 400 FEET.
EST 88MPH. LARGE COTTONWOOD IN FAIR TO GOOD HEALTH SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK
EST 90MPH. ONE HOME LOST ABOUT HALF OF THE ROOF AND IT WAS BLOWN INTO THE BACKYARD AND ALLEY. SEVERAL TREES DOWN AND UPROOTED IN TOWN.
EST 105MPH. SEVERAL LARGE HEALTHY SOFT WOOD TREES SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK OR UPROOTED AT THE ARNOLD CEMETARY.
EST 80 MPH. CORNER OF A ROOF ON A WELL BUILT NEW HOME PEELED BACK. DECKING WAS ALSO PEELED BACK. SOME SHINGLES WERE MISSING FROM THE PEAK OF THE ROOF.
RELAYED BY EMERGENCY MANAGER THAT A PIVOT WAS TURNED OVER, LOTS OF TREES WERE DOWN, POWER LINES DOWN AND A QUARTER OF A BUILDING THAT WAS 100FT BY 80 FT DESTROYED.
ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS OF 85 MPH. CORREGATED STEEL BUILDING BLOWN APART AND POWERLINES DOWN.

EST 88MPH. TREE UPROOTED AT THE CATHOLIC CHURCH IN SOUTH ANSELMO. SEVERAL OTHER TREES HAD LARGE LIMBS UP TO 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER BROKEN. SOME TREES UPROOTED BUT DID NOT APPEAR TO BE FULLY HEALTHY. PART OF A LARGE SHED LOST A PORTION OF THE ROOF.
